transcript: A woman finds herself in a futuristic prison cell, and she's determined to do everything she can to survive. The United Kingdom is undergoing a civil war. The leader of the UK Liberation Army, or UKLA, Citizen General Zakarian, is fighting against corruption, greed, xenophobia, and racism that he claims is plaguing the government. A woman wakes up inside a white room. When it seems like she's given up thoughts of escape, a voice offers her a chocolate bar. She hesitates, but quickly devours it after the voice confirms it has no nuts. The voice asks what she remembers, and the woman replies that she was delivering a document. She introduces herself as Ruth, who does clerical duties at the facility. Moments later, the temperature rises dramatically, causing Ruth to collapse. She asks what the voice wants, and it replies, saying that it's interested in learning what they do in the facility. Ruth hears the voice suffering from hacking coughs, and she deflects the question by showing concern. Within moments, the temperature suddenly drops. The voice warns Ruth of hypothermia, suggesting she remove her sweaty clothes. Ruth heeds the suggestion. Moreover, she begins running vigorously in place until she collapses. Later, Ruth requests for her clothes to be dried. Promptly, a container slides open, and the voice tells her to put the clothes inside. The voice praises her will to survive, so she asks what it would have done if she died. The container slides open again, but this time, she's horrified at what's inside. Inside is a dismembered hand wearing a gold watch, which Ruth recognizes. She cradles the hand, tearfully bringing it close to her face. The voice informs her that savagery is always an option. Ruth insists that she's just an errand girl, ignorant of the facility's activities. Deeming her worthless, the chamber begins emitting an ear-piercing noise. Desperate, Ruth cries out, promising she'll talk and immediately the screeching stops. Moments later, the face of Citizen General Zakarian appears on a screen. She divulges hearing rumors that the facility is developing something for the war, though never hearing any details. Unconcerned, Zakarian says there's nothing the government can do that can turn the tides, before erupting into fits of coughing. Ruth blames him for turning the country into a war zone, indiscriminately killing civilians and even his own men. If it were true, Zakarian asks her why people are joining his cause. She replies simply with, to stand together. Zakarian scoffs and states that the country can't stand foreigners, much less stand together. Before disappearing, he says that the UK LA chose violent acts because it was the only way to be noticed. Later, Ruth wonders how Zakarian lives with all his atrocities. The citizen general confides that it hurts, but enemies are enemies, and he's willing to kill for his kill first beliefs zakarian then mentions dr chrysler and ruth confirms that he's the facility's head weaponry engineer the rebel leader comments that his big project couldn't just be the white chamber accusing ruth of holding back information suddenly the chamber goes dark and ruth convulses in pain as she's electrocuted afterward one side of the chamber disappears and the citizen general instructs her to approach it. In agony, Ruth protests that she can't. Promptly, a drop of acid falls on her, and Zakarian repeats his instruction. Fearing more acid, Ruth plods towards the dark glass. The moment her face touches it, the glass lights up, revealing a disfigured woman. Terrified, Ruth recoils to the opposite wall. Zakarian describes the disfigured woman as the fruits of the country's government and cruelty suddenly the glass vanishes and the rabid woman charges at ruth frantic ruth avoids her attacker the rabid woman knocks her over and bites her upper back while the zombie-like woman relishes ruth grabs the dismembered finger on the floor and drives it into the woman's eyes ruth trudges to the monitor and declares that she has nothing of value to offer him. Then, she hears a sound. Five days earlier, on day one, the real Ruth entered the facility and an old man wearing a gold watch, Dr. Ed Chrysler, introduced himself and Sandra. Soon, Dr. L. Chrysler appeared and shook Ruth's hand. Immediately, L. asked if she handled knots recently, which Ruth confirmed. In gasping breaths, L. instructed them to meet her downstairs and hurried to her EpiPen. Ed calmly told Ruth to wash her hands thoroughly going forward. Shortly thereafter, the chemical engineering development team is reminded that their activities are classified. Then Elle, the head scientist, formally introduced Ruth to the team. Later, Elle unveiled to Ruth the reason for all the secrecy. On the monitor is Zakarian, confined within the White Chamber. Turning on communications, El greeted him and thanked him for helping them develop a weapon against the civil war he started. Hearing his captor's presence, Zakarian asked for a deodorant, since he's been wearing the same clothes for days. Before going down to the Chamber's control panel, El notified Ruth that she must never let her identity be known to the test subject. Ruth watched as El operated the controls, causing water to pour on one section of the chamber. Delighted, Zakarian showered and thanked them afterwards. Later, he's given food, but he's wary of poison. A voice assured him that they need him alive, not dead. Hearing this, Zakarian wolfed down the meal and even expected dessert. Moments later, he started acting restless. Again, water started pouring. First, freezing cold, then scorching hot. Both both times ed reported that zakarian's brain isn't registering the extreme temperatures at this al ends the experiment she turned to ruth asking how much she was informed about the facility the newcomer responded that all she knows is that they do military related chemical testing upstairs ruth is briefed that their purpose is to develop a stimulant with minimal side effects for their soldiers. However, a substance like that can only be made through rigorous testing, and that's what they are doing scientific tests. Sandra then brought over their concoction, Argo, named after its silver appearance. Ruth deduced that Zakarian's earlier behavior was because his food was laced with Argo. Elle notified her that the National Security Council has insisted they use Zakion as their test subject, asking Ruth if she's unsettled by that information. Her response is that the war has to end through whatever means necessary. Elle then handed her over to Sandra to learn the exact recipe for Argo. Afterwards, they clean up when Ruth spotted a bottle of Malice Mercury, a slow-acting poison used to assassinate their Prime Minister. Sandra reminded her to be careful, so Ruth cautiously put it back in place. Excusing herself, Sandra instructed Ruth to wait until someone else relieves her. Before leaving, the senior scientist informed her about the panic button for future emergencies. Later, Ruth noticed that the security monitors are inoperational. She walked over to the control panel to personally check on Zakarian. Suddenly, Elle shoved her aside, rebuking Ruth for almost showing her face. Unnerved, Ruth shakily reported that the monitors weren't working, so the head scientist rushed upstairs. L reactivates the monitors to find Zakarian still high off the Argo. Ruth asked what else needs to be improved, as their current formulation seems to be perfect. L coolly responded that safety can only be ascertained after experimenting with multiple batches, then dismissed the newcomer. Meanwhile, Zakarian asserted that drugs won't make him divulge anything, and if they won't let him sleep, he can work around it. The prisoner then slammed his head against the wall, falling unconscious. On day two, Zakarian is zapped awake, then given food. Upon his refusal to eat, gas is pumped inside. Outside, Elle entrusted the control panel to Ruth, but the newcomer protested, citing lack of training. Her boss countered that she was confident enough to attempt it yesterday, and then reassured Ruth that she will be guided accordingly. The head scientist explained that the gas contains an improved batch of Argo. Inside, contrary to his earlier defiant attitude, Zakarian happily complied by lying on the floor. Ruth is told to zap their test subject with incrementally increasing intensity. The scientists were happy to see that Zakarian can disregard voltages that could incapacitate most humans. Elle congratulated the citizen general on his impressive pain threshold, and he replied by correctly guessing her identity as Dr. Chrysler, head weaponry engineer. Zakarian expressed his disdain for using their skills to build a torture device. Upon hearing this, El commanded Ruth to zap Zakarian with an intensity that would cause organ damage. The newcomer hesitated but complied. Still, Zakarian continued, saying El only joined the war after losing a loved one. Between bouts of electrocution, he insulted this person, calling them a coward. Zakarian wished it was he who killed them. He belittled their death, stating it's just another body on the road to liberty. Ed addressed Ruth, telling her the test is done, but Elle disagreed. Despite this, the electrocution stopped. Elle is outraged, accusing Ruth of disobeying an order and threatening her with the consequences of treason. As she barked at Ruth, Ed shouted back, revealing that it was him that ended her test. He declared that the experiment was a success, there was no need to continue. Before retreating, L mentioned Jamie, knowing Ed would understand. Dismayed, Ed went outside. Shortly thereafter, Ruth joined Ed and thanked him. He asked her why she's on their side, considering she's of color too. Ruth shared that she never experienced what the rebels are fighting against, explaining that she was fortunate enough to have grown in an environment that allowed her to flourish regardless of skin color. And ultimately, she's there in an attempt to contribute in finishing the war. Ed then apologized for Elle, his daughter. No matter how professional they need to be, they still have feelings. He revealed that Jamie is his son and showed Ruth an ivory tusk, a gift from Jamie. Moreover, Elle also lost her husband, David, in the war. Two of the most valuable people in Elle's life, killed in battle, fought on opposing sides. Ed explained that even among family, people fight for different things. Later, Sandra announced her presence and asked Elle if there are any more tests planned. Apprehensively, Elle asked if she's a monster. She wondered if letting Zakarian go is the right thing to do, if taking the high road would open up another way to end the war. Sandra confided that she used to ask herself that too however she's certain that a warmonger can't be allowed to control the country even if it means resorting to violent acts to prevent it hearing this l's conviction is restored and tells sandra to continue monitoring the test subject outside after noticing the planes overhead ed remarked that their enemies are approaching meanwhile l comes to terms with the realities of her work she's just scared as everyone else, but must put on a brave face in front of her colleagues. Later, Zakarian begged for help. Unconcerned, Sandra stated that he's merely going through withdrawal, and the symptoms will pass. Ruth watched in silent horror while everyone else was indifferent to the man's screams. On day three, Ed told Ruth to feed Zakarian, stating his system needs to be cleared for the next batch. Ruth informed Zakarian that it's been 24 hours since his last meal, urging him to eat. He requested that they season the food with more Argo, and Ruth told him not to worry for the pain will pass eventually. He ate, but got sick moments later. Just then, El arrived, informing them that they have an additional test subject. Dejected, Ruth looked on as the prisoner was brought in. Soon, Zakarian woke up to see Anya, his lover, also confined inside the White Chamber. The Citizen General was confident they would be rescued, certain because he's a Zakarian. Anya retorted that he's a Zakarian that was about to abandon his brothers, but he maintained that they will not forsake their brother, their blood. Anya declared her love for Darren, and in turn, Darren's Zakarian promised that they will escape together. El interjected that she can't allow them to escape, they still have work to do. Before long, gas flooded Anya's side of the chamber. Soon, she began laughing in pleasure. Outside, Sandra suggested to test her pain threshold. In moments, acid began to drip on Anya, though she takes no notice. Ruth objected, stating they are adding unnecessary variables to the experiment. Sandra corrected her, defining what they're doing as tests, not experiments. As Elle walked away, Ruth demanded to know what will happen to the subjects once testing is complete. Elle responded that their fate is none of their concern. On day five, Ruth informed Sandra that the subjects declined the food, requesting instead for pure Argo. Soon, Elle returned from her meeting and cheerfully informed them that the National Security Council was satisfied with her product. Lab testing is complete, and they will commence production of number 47 for field testing. Hearing this, Ruth is confused why they would approve one of the earliest formulas, and Elle questioned her professionalism. Feeling tensions rise, Ed reminded Ruth that soon everything will be over. She can finally go home proud to have contributed to a safer future for her family. Ruth apologized and L instructed her to feed the remaining samples of number 58 to Anya. Ruth objected, citing they were just given a big dose. L told her it was an order, not a request. Ruth whispered an apology as she gave the manic prisoner more drugs. Moments later, L showed her face to Zakaria. She introduced herself and then told the citizen general of his role in her brother and husband's death. Surprised at hearing that her husband was on his side, he asked for his name, asserting that he knows each soldier that died for their cause. Upon mentioning David, Zakarian proceeded to name his parents, surprising El. He continued, disclosing that he personally called David's parents, commanding him as one of his finest commanders. Zakarian then asked El if she's valued by the council or is just another cog in the machine. Moreover, by helping the government, she's actively fighting against David's beliefs, diminishing David's sacrifice. Unable to muster a meaningful reply, El resorted to savagery. She made Anya's side of the chamber visible again, and Zakarian is forced to watch as Anya, driven to frenzy by the drugs, resorted to self-cannibalism. Later, done with their final Argo batch, Elle thanked the members for participating in the tests. As Ruth remained silent, Sandra told her that their test subjects are guilty of treason and will be dealt with accordingly. Soon, Elle spotted Ruth by the control panel and rushed down to stop her. There, Ruth expressed that the past few days have shattered her notions of civilization, and for Elle in particular, they were not tests, but a thinly disguised act of revenge. Elle countered that they're all equally responsible and commanded her to step away from the controls. Ruth refused, adamant that the world needs to know the inhumane activities of the government. To drive her point, Ruth said neither Jamie nor David would have wanted this, calling Elle a disgrace to their memory. Infuriated, Elle stabbed Ruth with a scalpel. When they broke apart, Ruth collapsed, bloodied. Sandra asked what she's done, but all the head scientist could muster was incoherent babbling. Ed rushed to Ruth's aid. He propped her up beside the control panel and maintained pressure on the wound. He stated that help will arrive shortly, while Sandra condemned the action as murder. Desperate, Elle defended herself by saying Ruth couldn't be allowed to leak any information regarding their activities and undermine their efforts at peace. To placate the agitated woman, Sandra offered to dispose of the bloody clothes and scalpel. Soon, the gravity of her actions register and Elle panics. Upstairs, Sandra pressed the panic button. As Ed and Elle discussed calling for medical assistance, Ruth spent her final moments releasing Zakarian from the White Chamber. The scientists are defenseless against the drug-enhanced rebel, making quick work of the scientists. After inhaling another handful of Argo, he jumped several floors down to finish off Elle. Ed made his last stand and drove his ivory necklace into Zakarian's neck, but the aggressive man barely noticed and proceeded instead to attack Ed. Elle fainted at the horrific sight and eventually, Zakarian succumbed to the blood loss. Upstairs, Sandra regained consciousness in time to open the doors, expecting assistance. Instead, she got shot as UKLA rebels swarmed the facility. Soon, a masked man entered and cradled Zakarian's dying body. Narek Zakarian took off his mask as his brother died in his arms. In the present, Elle exits the White Chamber, armed to the bones. She surveys the area and sees multiple UKLA soldiers, as well as Narek. She approaches him, wondering why he allowed Anya to be killed. The Citizen General explains that Anya was an obstruction to the UKLA, convincing his brother to abandon the fight. Realizing that Narek knew the entire time who she was, Elle resigns herself to be killed. She claims responsibility for torturing Darren and ending an innocent woman. Having said that, El urges Narek to kill her. Instead, Narek gives her clothes, telling her there's work to be done. He shows her the Argo and assumes that this was what they were developing. El denies it, still adhering to protocol. Tired of lies, the Citizen General throws nuts at her, amazed that she can survive the White Chamber but not peanuts. Hearing her last gasp, Narek leisurely administers an EpiPen. Later, the rebel leader tells Elle that she knows what David expects her to do. After Narek leaves, Elle meekly states that she needs more coloring agent. She goes to the chemical cabinet and takes out the bottle of Malice Mercury. Deciding to be a survivor, Elle's voice grows steady as she proceeds to make a poisonous batch of Argo. Subscribe to watch more videos like this. Turn on notifications and leave a like to help the channel out. Thank you for watching.

Having said that, El urges Narek to kill her. Instead, Narek gives her clothes, telling her there's work to be done. He shows her the Argo and assumes that this was what they were developing. El denies it, still adhering to protocol. Tired of lies, the Citizen General throws nuts at her, amazed that she can survive the White Chamber but not peanuts. Hearing her last gasp, Narek leisurely administers an EpiPen. Later, the rebel leader tells Elle that she knows what David expects her to do. After Narek leaves, Elle meekly states that she needs more coloring agent. She goes to the chemical cabinet and takes out the bottle of Malice Mercury. Deciding to be a survivor, Elle's voice grows steady as she proceeds to make a poisonous
